message:
fix(ui): allow search-as-you-type to supersede in-flight searches

The debounced search-as-you-type was blocked from firing while any
search was already in-flight (the Tick handler checked
`!self.search_in_flight` before dispatching SearchRequested). This
meant that when the initial empty-query search at startup took 60+
seconds on a large index (41K conversations), the user's typed query
was queued but wouldn't execute until the initial search finished.

Root cause: the guard `if !self.search_in_flight` at the Tick handler's
debounce check prevented overlapping searches entirely. While this
avoided redundant backend work, it created catastrophic UX lag — the
user types a query and sees no response for over a minute.

Fix: remove the search_in_flight gate. The generation counter
(search_generation) already handles stale results correctly — when a
new search fires, it increments the generation, and when the old
search completes with a stale generation, its results are silently
discarded (line 14180: `if generation != self.search_generation`).

This is safe because:
- The backend search is async (ftui::Cmd::task), so multiple searches
  can be in-flight concurrently without blocking the UI thread
- Stale results are always ignored via the generation check
- The search_in_flight flag is still set/cleared correctly for the
  loading spinner and status display

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
